extra.message: User input from request.form['expression'] is passed directly to eval() on line 120. An attacker can execute arbitrary Python code by submitting malicious expressions, achieving full remote code execution on the server.

cwe: CWE-94: Improper Control of Generation of Code ('Code Injection')

extra.message: User input from request.form['address'] is concatenated directly into a shell command string passed to os.popen() (via 'rp') on line 140: rp("nslookup " + address). An attacker can inject arbitrary OS commands using shell metacharacters such as '; rm -rf /' or '| cat /etc/passwd'.

cwe: CWE-78: Improper Neutralization of Special Elements used in an OS Command ('OS Command Injection')

extra.message: User-supplied XML from request.form['xml'] is parsed using lxml.etree.XMLParser with no_network=False and dtd_validation=True on line 157-159. External entity resolution is enabled, allowing an attacker to read arbitrary local files (e.g., /etc/passwd) or perform server-side request forgery via crafted XXE payloads like <!DOCTYPE foo [<!ENTITY xxe SYSTEM "file:///etc/passwd">]>.

cwe: CWE-611: Improper Restriction of XML External Entity Reference

extra.message:
User input from request.form['name'] is interpolated into a template string using Python string formatting on line 189 (name = '
Hello %s!

' %(request.form['name'])), which is then passed to render_template_string() on line 205. This enables Server-Side Template Injection (SSTI). An attacker can inject Jinja2 template expressions like {{config}} or {{''.__class__.__mro__[1].__subclasses__()}} to achieve remote code execution.

cwe: CWE-94: Improper Control of Generation of Code ('Code Injection')

extra.message: The result of eval(expression) on line 120 is concatenated directly into the HTML response string without any output encoding or escaping. Even if eval were replaced with a safe evaluator, the output would still allow reflected XSS since user-controlled content is rendered as raw HTML.

cwe: CWE-79: Improper Neutralization of Input During Web Page Generation ('Cross-site Scripting')

extra.message: The output of the OS command (which includes the user-supplied address) on line 140 is concatenated directly into the HTML response without escaping. An attacker can inject HTML/JavaScript via the 'address' form field, leading to reflected cross-site scripting.

cwe: CWE-79: Improper Neutralization of Input During Web Page Generation ('Cross-site Scripting')

extra.message: Parsed XML output on line 169 is concatenated directly into the HTML response string without output encoding. If the parsed XML contains user-controlled content, it will be rendered as raw HTML, enabling cross-site scripting.

cwe: CWE-79: Improper Neutralization of Input During Web Page Generation ('Cross-site Scripting')

extra.message: The Flask secret_key is hardcoded as 'Harry Potter And The Deathly Hallows' on line 15. This key is used to sign session cookies. An attacker who knows this key can forge arbitrary session data (e.g., set logged_in=True), bypassing authentication entirely.

User passwords are stored in plaintext on line 62: User(username=request.form['username'], password=request.form['password']). The User model (line 26) stores password as a plain db.String(80) with no hashing. Passwords are also compared in plaintext on line 50 via filter_by(password=passw). This exposes all user credentials if the database is compromised.

cwe: CWE-256: Plaintext Storage of a Password

extra.message: The /dashboard route (line 79-106) does not check session['logged_in'] before rendering the dashboard page. Any unauthenticated user can access the dashboard and all vulnerability endpoints (lookup, evaluate, xml, sayhi) which also lack authentication checks.

cwe: CWE-306: Missing Authentication for Critical Function

extra.message: The /evaluate route (line 111-130) which allows arbitrary code execution via eval() has no authentication check. Any unauthenticated user can execute arbitrary Python code on the server.

cwe: CWE-306: Missing Authentication for Critical Function

extra.message: The /lookup route (line 132-150) which allows OS command injection has no authentication check. Any unauthenticated user can execute arbitrary OS commands on the server.

cwe: CWE-306: Missing Authentication for Critical Function

extra.message: The /xml route (line 152-181) which parses user-supplied XML with external entity resolution enabled has no authentication check. Any unauthenticated user can exploit XXE to read server files.

cwe: CWE-306: Missing Authentication for Critical Function

extra.message: The /sayhi route (line 185-205) which is vulnerable to SSTI has no authentication check. Any unauthenticated user can exploit server-side template injection to achieve RCE.

cwe: CWE-306: Missing Authentication for Critical Function

extra.message: Flask debug mode is enabled on line 208 (app.debug = True). This exposes the Werkzeug interactive debugger, which allows arbitrary code execution if accessed, and leaks detailed stack traces and source code to attackers.

cwe: CWE-215: Insertion of Sensitive Information Into Debugging Code

extra.message: The pickle module is imported on line 1 along with base64 decode/encode functions on line 2. While no explicit pickle.loads() call is visible in the current code, the import of pickle alongside b64decode suggests infrastructure for insecure deserialization. The glossary template (line 37-38) describes 'Pickle Code Execution' as a feature of this application, indicating pickle deserialization may be exposed or intended.

The Flask application is configured to listen on all network interfaces (host='0.0.0.0') on line 211. Combined with debug mode being enabled, this exposes the Werkzeug debugger to the entire network, allowing remote code execution via the debugger console.
